question: Donna's Dad decided not to drive the Great Ocean Road himself because

options.A: He realized he wouldn't enjoy the views as much.
options.B: He thought it would be too tiring for him.
options.C: He discovered the bus would be a cheaper option.
options.D: He found out the route was very challenging.

question: When Donna saw a kangaroo along the route, she was

options.A: worried that it might approach her.
options.B: amazed at the size of it.
options.C: sad that it didn't stay with them long.
options.D: disappointed that she had forgotten her camera.

question: Donna says that the koala bears they saw were

options.A: responsible for damage to the trees.
options.B: even more attractive than people had told her.
options.C: more common than she'd expected.
options.D: very skilled at hiding away from tourists.

question: What was Donna's opinion of the place where they stayed?

options.A: She found it was less comfortable than she'd hoped.
options.B: She liked the fact that it was convenient for sightseeing.
options.C: She enjoyed hearing the sounds of nature as she slept.
options.D: She was disappointed there was no restaurant nearby.

question: What might Donna write in her blog during the trip?

options.A: The bus we're traveling on is pretty comfortable...
options.B: We can see quite a lot as we drive along...
options.C: Yesterday we went to see some huge rocks...
options.D: I wasn't looking forward to camping... but now I feel silly.

question: Jack wasn't keen to mention his whale-watching trip to Olivia because

options.A: he thought her trip sounded a lot more exciting.
options.B: he'd read some negative reports about where he was going.
options.C: he wasn't sure if his dad had definitely arranged it.
options.D: he didn't know whether she was very interested in whales.

question: On board the whale-watching boat, Jack

options.A: was proud to be the first person to see a whale.
options.B: began to worry that they might all be disappointed.
options.C: tried not to get excited when anyone saw something.
options.D: was glad he'd persuaded his dad to come with him.

question: When Jack saw the minke whale, he was

options.A: surprised at how close it came to the boat.
options.B: amazed that it was so much bigger than he'd imagined.
options.C: impressed that it moved through the water so easily.
options.D: delighted to see it had arrived with several others.

question: Jack suggests minke whales appeared in the same area as the boat because

options.A: they knew there were plenty of fish there.
options.B: they were attracted by the arrival of the visitors.
options.C: they didn't have to compete for food with seabirds.
options.D: they preferred how deep the water was there.

question: What would Jack text to his grandparents about the whales?

options.A: I'll send you my video of the group of whales...
options.B: Dad said he'd really wanted to go to Canada...
options.C: I must take you up to the cliffs to look for whales...
options.D: You were so sure our whale-watching trip would be a success...

question: The food at Freshly

options.A: is grown by the staff.
options.B: comes from places in or close to Brighton.
options.C: comes from all over the world.
options.D: comes all over Britain.

question: Where did the owner start to train as a chef?

options.A: at junior school
options.B: at university
options.C: at home with the help of her family
options.D: in a hotel

question: How did Jane feel while she was training to be a chef?

options.A: She was really tired but felt positive about the experience.
options.B: She wanted to give up because of the pressure.
options.C: She wasn't sure if she would be successful.
options.D: She found it quite easy to finish the training.

question: What is said about Freshly restaurant?

options.A: The head waiter is in charge of the menu.
options.B: The staff often change jobs because they like to do different things.
options.C: It is a big and fancy restaurant with a great view.
options.D: The staff have a very good relationship with each other.

question: What would Peter say about Freshly restaurant?

options.A: Freshly restaurant may serve tasty food but it is extremely pricey - not worth it.
options.B: A great effort from a successful young chef who supports local products.
options.C: A famous chef who tried rather unsuccessfully to set up a different kind of restaurant.
options.D: The cheerful staff and nice location are the only things that are worth mentioning.

question: What helped Ana stop being scared of singing to an audience?

options.A: practicing her singing with a band
options.B: asking a teacher to stand on stage with her
options.C: being in a drama group at school
options.D: making sure she was not in the front row

question: How was the songwriting course useful for Ana?

options.A: She was able to meet some well-known singers.
options.B: The teacher's attitude gave her an idea for a song.
options.C: She learned a lot from other writers on the course.
options.D: The teacher gave her advice about the music business.

question: Ana decided to work with Joss Alton because

options.A: he promised to let her choose which songs to sing.
options.B: he offered to help her put on a concert.
options.C: she liked some other singers that he worked with.
options.D: he owned a company in her hometown.

question: Ana most enjoys writing songs that

options.A: she is sure her fans will like it.
options.B: deal with difficult times.
options.C: her brother can sing with his band.
options.D: help people remember their own experiences.

question: What would Ana write to fans in her blog?

options.A: Listening to my parents playing music...
options.B: Writing songs just gets easier with practice...
options.C: I loved playing the concert in Dublin...
options.D: I read what people write about me online...
